title: US forces enter Iran by..?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Only US military personnel who deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran for operational purposes (e.g., military, humanitarian, etc.) will qualify. Pilots who are shot down, or other cases in which US military personnel do not deliberately enter the terrestrial territory of Iran, will not qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US and Iran agreed to a conditional two-week ceasefire on April 7, 2026, following weeks of intense airstrikes under Operation Epic Fury that began February 28, with US and Israeli forces targeting Iranian missile sites, air defenses, naval assets, and command facilities, destroying over 90% of key capabilities per Pentagon assessments. Offensive operations have ceased immediately, though US forces—including up to 50,000 troops in the region—remain positioned for potential resumption if negotiations in Islamabad fail to secure Strait of Hormuz access. Limited special operations insertions occurred earlier for pilot rescues near Iranian cities, but no sustained ground combat presence has materialized amid de-escalation signals. Traders weigh compliance risks and diplomatic outcomes through late April against the ceasefire's fragility.

title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another, or for an official ceasefire agreement to be otherwise confirmed to have been reached by an overwhelming consensus of media reporting.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, an overwhelming consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A two-week US-Iran ceasefire took effect April 8, 2026, after President Trump's April 7 announcement, brokered by Pakistan amid 38 days of US-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian nuclear sites, missile facilities, and the Strait of Hormuz blockade. Conditioned on Tehran reopening the key oil transit route, the truce enables talks in Islamabad starting Friday, led by Vice President Vance on a US 15-point framework demanding nuclear curbs and sanctions relief against Iran's 10-point counterproposal. Israel endorses the pause but pressed strikes in Lebanon, sparking compliance disputes and escalation risks. Traders eye negotiation outcomes by April 21 for potential extensions or renewed hostilities.

title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 between Paris Saint-Germain FC and Liverpool FC.

markets.question: Will Paris Saint-Germain FC win on 2026-04-08?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 8, 2026
If Paris Saint-Germain FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain secured a commanding 2-0 victory over Liverpool in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg at Parc des Princes, with Désiré Doué opening the scoring in the 11th minute via a deflection and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia adding a solo stunner in the 65th, driving trader consensus to a 100% implied probability on the PSG win outcome. As defending champions, PSG dominated with 74% possession and 18 shots to Liverpool's three, exposing Arne Slot's experimental back-five formation and the visitors' toothless attack amid recent out-of-form struggles. While the market reflects the official full-time result per UEFA, rare scenarios like goal-line technology review or administrative reversal could theoretically challenge resolution, though Liverpool now faces a steep aggregate deficit ahead of the Anfield second leg.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's trader consensus edge at 15.7% stems from their unbeaten UEFA qualifying Group E dominance—five wins, one draw, +19 goal difference—capped by March friendlies like a 3-0 Serbia rout, building on Euro 2024 glory and Lamine Yamal's breakout. France trails tightly at 13.9% after mirroring Spain's near-perfect Group D run (+12 GD), fueled by Kylian Mbappé's firepower, while England's 11.3% reflects a flawless 8-0 Group K sweep (+22 GD). Argentina (9%) holds firm as defending champions atop CONMEBOL standings, but Brazil's (8.6%) slide to fifth tempers South American hype. With qualifiers wrapping March 31 and the 48-team field finalized via play-offs like Sweden's Poland upset, the expanded format's group draw opens knockout paths, keeping Europe's golden generations in a razor-thin race.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: The United States and Iran reached a two-week ceasefire agreement on April 7, brokered by Pakistan, pausing hostilities amid ongoing negotiations set to begin in Islamabad this Friday; this follows six weeks of escalation since late February, marked by US-Israel airstrikes on Iranian nuclear infrastructure, ballistic missile sites, and naval assets, countered by Iranian missile barrages on Israel and Gulf states. Israel backs the truce but remains skeptical, citing unmet war aims and continuing limited operations against Hezbollah proxies in Lebanon, while President Trump hails it as a strategic victory with the Strait of Hormuz reopening. Traders eye negotiation progress, potential extensions, or fragile truce breakdowns as pivotal for assessing de-escalation timelines.

title: The Masters - Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler leads trader consensus at 13.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Masters, buoyed by his world No. 1 status, two prior green jackets, and consistent 2026 form including a win at The American Express and top-25s elsewhere, despite skipping the Valero Texas Open for family. The field remains tightly bunched with Jon Rahm (7.9%), Bryson DeChambeau (6.5%), Rory McIlroy (6.5% as defending champion post-back spasms recovery), and Xander Schauffele (6.3%) close behind, reflecting DeChambeau's recent LIV dominance (two wins), Rahm's major pedigree, McIlroy's Augusta affinity, and a deep 91-player roster amid Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson absences. Favorable dry weather forecasts for firm, fast greens amplify ball-striking edges while underscoring upset potential in this unpredictable major.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom commands trader consensus at 27.5% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, propelled by March polls showing him trouncing Kamala Harris in a California primary matchup and tying for second in New Hampshire, signaling strong early-state viability and fundraising prowess as a term-limited executive with national profile from 2024 anti-Trump efforts. Progressive firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.6% on youth appeal and grassroots energy, while Georgia Sen. Jon Ossoff's 6.5% reflects swing-state incumbency and bipartisan moderation. This wide-open field post-Harris's 2024 defeat lacks a clear frontrunner; consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm performances for governors and senators, early fundraising reports, DNC endorsements, and scandal avoidance ahead of Iowa and New Hampshire caucuses and primaries.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads trader consensus at 18.8% implied probability for the 2028 presidential winner, bolstered by recent GOP straw polls like CPAC (53%) and JL Partners (53% over Marco Rubio), positioning him as the Republican heir apparent amid President Trump's term limits. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 16.5%, buoyed by his national profile as a Democratic anti-Trump figurehead and early primary polling edges. The tight race reflects an open field ahead of 2026 midterms, which could elevate governors like Newsom or Rubio through battleground performances, while economic trends, Trump endorsements, or scandals might create separation in this highly fluid, skin-in-the-game market.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President J.D. Vance trails HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. closely in trader consensus for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, with Senator Marco Rubio third, reflecting early jockeying in a wide-open post-Trump field constrained by term limits. RFK Jr.'s lead stems from his high-visibility MAHA agenda targeting chronic disease, microplastics, and food additives, which has drawn independents and base enthusiasm despite GOP pushback on vaccine policies and Trump's glyphosate executive order; his recent campaign swings with Republican lawmakers in swing states like Pennsylvania, Michigan, and Arizona signal presidential ambitions. Vance leverages VP incumbency and CPAC/New Hampshire straw poll dominance but stays coy amid foreign policy strains. Midterm outcomes and cabinet successes could widen gaps before primaries.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude futures plummeted 16% to settle near $94 per barrel on April 8, 2026, erasing gains from the prior day's surge above $117, as President Trump's announced two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire eased geopolitical risk premiums despite reports of an Iranian strike on Saudi Arabia's East-West pipeline. This volatility capsizes trader sentiment after Q1 price spikes from military actions and sanctions boosting U.S. exports. Bearish fundamentals include multi-week EIA crude inventory builds to near three-year highs and OPEC+ April production increases, contrasting EIA's forecast for a Q2 Brent peak at $115/bbl before surpluses emerge. Key catalysts ahead: EIA weekly inventories April 9 and OPEC Monthly Oil Market Report April 13.

title: FC Barcelona vs. Club Atlético de Madrid
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 between FC Barcelona and Club Atlético de Madrid.

markets.question: Will FC Barcelona win on 2026-04-08?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 8, 2026
If FC Barcelona wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid's 2-0 victory over FC Barcelona in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final first leg at Camp Nou has locked in trader consensus at near-certainty for the visitors, reflecting the final whistle outcome where goals from Julián Álvarez and Alexander Sørloth proved decisive. Barcelona, leading La Liga by seven points after their recent 2-1 league win over Atlético four days prior, were reduced to 10 men late in the first half via Pau Cubarsí's red card, hampering their comeback bid despite home advantage and superior domestic form. Simeone's defensive resilience and clinical counterattacks exploited the numerical edge, with the market now resolved barring extraordinary appeals or administrative reversals, though such challenges remain highly improbable.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 40.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA Championship, fueled by their league-best 63-16 record and an 18-1 surge over the last 19 games, including blowout wins over the Lakers and Jazz, positioning them to clinch the No. 1 Western Conference seed ahead of the April 18 playoffs start. San Antonio Spurs follow at 15.3% after a 60-19 mark highlighted by Stephon Castle's triple-double in a 115-102 rout of the 76ers on April 6, showcasing Victor Wembanyama's growth and a potent young core despite an overtime loss to Denver. Boston Celtics (11.3%) hold the East's No. 2 seed at 54-25 with Jaylen Brown's 35-point explosion versus Charlotte on April 7, while Denver Nuggets (10.5%) ride a nine-game win streak including a 136-134 OT thriller over San Antonio. Detroit Pistons top the East at 57-22 but trail at 6.3% amid tougher title path projections versus Western depth.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 98% implied probability to no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting the Federal Reserve's steady 3.50%-3.75% target range from its March 17-18 session amid resilient economic indicators. March nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs—exceeding expectations—while unemployment dipped to 4.3%, signaling a robust labor market with no urgent need for policy shifts; the updated dot plot still projects one cut later in 2026. Fresh March FOMC minutes released April 8 underscore measured pacing on monetary policy. Scenarios challenging this positioning include hotter-than-expected CPI data or weakening jobless claims ahead of the meeting, though current trader sentiment prices low risk of deviation.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Péter Magyar at 68.5% to become Hungary's next prime minister following the April 12 parliamentary election, driven by recent opinion polls showing his Tisza party leading Fidesz-KDNP by an average of 10 points (49-39% per Poll of Polls) among decided voters, with surges like Medián's 58-35 Tisza advantage. This reflects opposition consolidation behind Magyar—a former Fidesz insider turned challenger—youth voter shifts amid economic discontent and frozen EU funds, widening leads reported last week by Reuters and Bloomberg. Viktor Orbán's 31.5% odds account for Fidesz's incumbency, rural strongholds, and advantages in the mixed-member proportional system with gerrymandered districts favoring the incumbent. The National Assembly majority will determine the president's appointment of the next prime minister, with outcomes hinging on turnout and undecideds.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's trader consensus lead at 29.5% implied probability stems from their unbeaten 8-0-0 league phase and a crucial late Havertz winner in their quarter-final first leg, while Bayern Munich's 28.5% reflects a gritty 2-1 victory over Real Madrid, elevating both above PSG's 21.5% despite the Parisians' defending champion status and firepower led by Mbappé. This tight bunching underscores the competitive knockout dynamics, with second-leg ties next week introducing home advantages, potential comebacks, and matchup uncertainties—Atletico Madrid (7.6%) and Barcelona (6.7%) lurk as spoilers amid no major injury disruptions, keeping the path to the Budapest final wide open for upsets in the single-elimination format.

title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Liverpool FC - More Markets
description: More markets for the UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 8 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: Spread: Paris Saint-Germain FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for April 8 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Paris Saint-Germain FC" if Paris Saint-Germain FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Liverpool FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain hold a scheduling edge entering their UEFA Champions League quarter-final against Liverpool, with Ligue 1 approving the postponement of their key Lens fixture to allow full rest between the April 8 first leg at Parc des Princes and April 14 return at Anfield. PSG's recent form remains strong atop Ligue 1, but injuries plague their squad: young midfielder Senny Mayulu sidelined with a right calf issue requiring two weeks' treatment, and winger Bradley Barcola out weeks with ankle ligament damage. Liverpool, leading the Premier League, face a grueling run including Premier League clashes on April 4 and 11 around the ties, testing their depth amid solid recent Champions League momentum from a 4-0 second-leg rout of Galatasaray. Home advantage in Paris favors PSG initially, while Anfield's atmosphere could prove decisive in the aggregate.

title: LoL: Ninjas in Pyjamas vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Ninjas in Pyjamas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 24.5 in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 5:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 25 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. markets.question: Game Handicap: NIP (-1.5) vs Invictus Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Ninjas in Pyjamas and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Ninjas in Pyjamas" if Ninjas in Pyjamas wins 2 or more games than Invictus Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Invictus Gaming enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 as the trader-favored side after both teams notched clean 2-0 sweeps in Week 1, with IG dominating their opener and NIP handling Team WE convincingly. IG's veteran roster—featuring jungler Wei, midlaner Breathe (with Rookie influences in recent play), and supports like Meiko—excels in macro execution and skirmish control under the current patch, giving them a historical edge in head-to-heads against NIP's rebuilding lineup of top import Hoya, mid Assum, and support Guwon. No reported injuries or roster changes disrupt preparations, but NIP's import synergy remains a question mark in double round-robin play where early momentum shapes playoff paths from Group Ascend.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US military strikes targeted Iranian military installations on Kharg Island, Iran's vital Persian Gulf oil export hub handling nearly 90% of its crude shipments, as recently as April 7 amid escalating tensions over Strait of Hormuz access and fees imposed by Tehran. Iranian state media released footage asserting full armed forces control, uninterrupted oil exports, and operational security following the attacks, which spared energy infrastructure. A two-week US-Iran ceasefire was announced April 6, with negotiations set to begin Friday in Islamabad; failure could prompt further airstrikes, blockade, or ground operations previously considered by the Trump administration to seize the island. No verified loss of control has occurred, though proxy threats and diplomatic posturing sustain market uncertainty.

title: Colombia Presidential Election
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election. 

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M narrowly ahead at 49.5% implied probability for Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round win, with Paloma Valencia close at 45.6% and Iván Cepeda Castro at 39.5%, reflecting a fiercely contested race amid fragmented opposition and no candidate polling above 42% in late-March surveys. The March 8 legislative elections delivered Historic Pact the largest congressional bloc but no majority, boosting Cepeda's left-wing base while highlighting right-wing division between Valencia's Centro Democrático and Abelardo de la Espriella; recent polls show Valencia surging on security messaging. President Petro's April 8 economic emergency declaration amplifies voter anxiety over stability and reforms, keeping odds tight. Endorsements, debates, or violence spikes could consolidate support and force a June 21 runoff.

title: Trump announces end of military operations against Iran by ...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announce that their military operations against Iran, initiated on February 28, 2026, have concluded by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying statements must clearly indicate that the operation has ended. Informal announcements, statements from unnamed sources or leaks will not qualify. 

Written public statements from Donald Trump (e.g. posts from his personal Truth Social account), will count. Videos posted on his social media accounts will also qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from the US government and/or its official representatives; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran on April 7, suspending U.S. airstrikes and offensive operations following recent strikes on military targets like Kharg Island, as Tehran agreed to negotiate in Islamabad starting Friday. This de-escalation signal follows Trump's April 1 claim that U.S. objectives in Operation Epic Fury are near completion, with Iran's navy, air force, missile program, and leadership significantly degraded since joint U.S.-Israel strikes began February 28. Pentagon leaders affirm forces remain ready to resume combat if talks falter or Iran violates terms. Trader consensus hinges on diplomatic outcomes during the truce, potential escalation signals, and whether a formal end to hostilities materializes amid fragile peace talks.

title: LoL: Dplus KIA vs Hanwha Life Esports (BO3) - LCK Rounds 1-2
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Dplus KIA" if Dplus KIA win the match against Hanwha Life Esports.

This market will resolve to "Hanwha Life Esports" if Hanwha Life Esports win the match against Dplus KIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 31.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 32 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 1 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game Handicap: HLE (-1.5) vs Dplus KIA (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Hanwha Life Esports" if Hanwha Life Esports wins 2 or more games than Dplus KIA in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Dplus KIA". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

In the upcoming match between Dplus KIA and Hanwha Life Esports in the LCK Rounds 1-2, initially scheduled for April 8 at 4:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 38 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the early LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2 group stage, Dplus KIA enters this BO3 matchup at second in standings after a commanding 2-0 sweep over KIWOOM DRX last week, bolstered by jungler Lucid's extension through 2027 and solid team synergy. Hanwha Life Esports, sitting fifth, kicked off with a dominant 2-0 win against HANJIN BRION two days ago, driven by standout jungler Kanavi's aggressive playmaking. No major roster changes or substitutions reported, with both squads adapting to the latest patch meta favoring skirmish-heavy comps; historical head-to-heads are split, heightening upset potential in this pivotal Week 2 clash amid tight playoff positioning.

title: Thunder vs. Clippers
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 8 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the Clippers win, the market will resolve to "Clippers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder (63-16), atop the Western Conference standings, head to Intuit Dome as heavy favorites against a shorthanded Los Angeles Clippers squad on the second night of a back-to-back. Thunder boast near-full health with Jalen Williams cleared from hamstring injury management and only rookie Thomas Sorber sidelined by a torn ACL, fueling their league-best record and momentum from prior blowout wins over LA (126-107 in November). Clippers counter with Kawhi Leonard available but missing Bradley Beal (hip), Darius Garland (toe), Isaiah Jackson (ankle), and Yanic Konan Niederhauser (foot), thinning their rotation amid late-season playoff positioning battles. Home-court edge and rest disadvantages for OKC could factor, but trader consensus reflects Thunder's superior form, depth, and head-to-head dominance.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs Team WE (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and Team WE in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against Team WE.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 2. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 2. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Top Esports and Team WE. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 2, or if Game 2 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 3. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 3.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and Team WE each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 3. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Top Esports and Team WE in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 8 at 7:00AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 1 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Top Esports holds a clear edge over Team WE in LPL Split 2 Group Ascend trader consensus, driven by sweeping H2H victories in Split 1 playoffs—3-2 in Knights Rivals and 3-0 in Knockout lower bracket—bolstered by midlaner Creme's consistent MVP-level play. TES sits at 1-1 (2-2 games) early in the double round-robin BO3 format with Fearless Draft, while WE struggles at 0-1 (0-2), hampered by a recent support swap to Erha amid bottom-of-group positioning. TES adapted post-naiyou's lifetime ban with jungler Tian's March integration and top ZUIAN promotion, enhancing macro execution against WE's import-heavy core of mid Karis and ADC About. Top-4 finish secures direct playoff berth, amplifying stakes in this Xi'an matchup.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 3 - April 10, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 3 12:00 PM ET to April 10, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.
