eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Elon Musk X post count from April 3 to April 10 heavily favors the 240-259 range at 38.5% implied probability, closely trailed by 220-239 at 33.5%, reflecting a nail-biting race shaped by his steady ~30 posts per day pace. With 179 qualifying main feed posts, quotes, and reposts tallied as of April 8 evening—86% through the period—the market hinges on the final April 9-10 stretch, where historical patterns like last week's 260-279 total suggest potential for a mild uptick. Key swing factors include SpaceX's dual launches scheduled for April 10, which could spark a posting surge amid high public buzz, versus quieter days if Musk prioritizes operations; traders with skin in the game remain split on whether event momentum breaks the ~240 deadlock.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the series finale at Rogers Centre, surging Los Angeles Dodgers (9-2, first in NL West) send Shohei Ohtani (1-0, 0.00 ERA in his first start) against Toronto Blue Jays right-hander Dylan Cease (0-0, 2.79 ERA), following LA's dominant 14-2 and 4-1 victories in the first two games behind strong outings from Yoshinobu Yamamoto and Jorbit Vivas. Dodgers' offense ranks top-tier at 6.55 runs per game, fueled by Ohtani's 42-game on-base streak, while Toronto (around 4-9, fourth in AL East) grapples with injuries to catcher Alejandro Kirk (thumb), infielder Addison Barger (ankle), and pitchers Shane Bieber (elbow) plus Yimi Garcia (elbow), contributing to 5.45 runs allowed per game and trader consensus favoring the visitors despite the home advantage.

title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: US and Israeli airstrikes since late February 2026 have targeted Iranian missile production, airbases, nuclear sites, and petrochemical facilities like Kharg Island, significantly degrading Tehran's military capabilities without any US ground invasion to date. The April 8 announcement of a 2-week ceasefire—brokered amid indirect negotiations via Pakistan—marks a pivotal de-escalation, with talks focusing on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, sanctions relief, and reconstruction as Trump met prior deadlines for escalation pauses. This diplomatic pivot, following Iran's rejections of earlier proposals, underpins the 68.5% "No" trader consensus, reflecting skepticism toward a costly full-scale invasion akin to Iraq amid positioned US forces and fragile truce risks ahead.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 97%, reflecting the Iranian regime's resilience despite early 2026 economic protests—sparked by rial collapse and quelled in January via deadly security force crackdowns killing thousands—and U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in early March that assassinated Supreme Leader Khamenei and top officials. Swift appointment of son Mojtaba Khamenei as successor, backed by Assembly of Experts consensus and interim councils, preserved command continuity, with Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps loyalty intact amid pro-regime demonstrations. Recent de-escalation via a Pakistan-mediated two-week ceasefire involving regional powers like Saudi Arabia and Egypt has stabilized tensions ahead of April 30 resolution. While improbable, a ceasefire breakdown, IRGC defection, or uncontrollable mass unrest could still prompt regime collapse.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona top the La Liga table with 73 points from 29 matches, seven points clear of Real Madrid after a dominant run including a 3-0 Copa del Rey win over Atlético Madrid on March 3—though Los Colchoneros advanced on aggregate to the final. Heading into the April 4 clash at the Metropolitano, trader consensus hinges on Barça's attacking firepower led by Lamine Yamal and Pedri amid Raphinha's fresh right hamstring injury from Brazil duty, potentially joining doubts over full-backs Koundé and Balde. Atlético, fourth in standings, face absences like goalkeeper Jan Oblak (muscle strain) and midfielder Pablo Barrios (thigh), but boast home strength under Simeone and momentum from a recent Madrid derby loss. Recent form favors Barça's away resilience, while Atlético's defensive setup could exploit any visitor fatigue.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the rubber match of their three-game series at PNC Park, Pittsburgh Pirates (6-3) host the San Diego Padres (5-6) with Mitch Keller (1-0, 1.50 ERA in two quality starts) facing Michael King (0-1, 3.38 ERA over 10 2/3 innings), a pivotal pitching duel amid early-season volatility. Pirates hold momentum after 5-0 and 7-1 wins in the first two games, bolstered by home-field advantage and strong recent home form, while Padres offense ranks 22nd in runs (13 total). San Diego's rotation remains depleted by injured list stints for Joe Musgrove (Tommy John recovery), Griffin Canning (Achilles), and others, straining depth; Pittsburgh misses INF Jared Triolo (knee, out weeks) but features healthier pitching staff. Weather at game time (12:35 p.m. ET) could factor with cool April conditions in Pittsburgh.
